name: workflow
description: BeSpunky ways-of-working: the process, order, and methodology of how work moves from idea to production — independent of what's being built. branch-and-release: the house git methodology — a one-way promotion pipeline across four branches (feature → development → staging → main, each a strict ancestor of the next so promotions are clean fast-forwards), unrelated work isolated in per-feature git worktrees off the integration branch (with the Nx-workspace-root override that stops tooling silently targeting the main tree), the relevance check that runs before touching any file, rebase-and-re-verify at the one divergence point (feature → development) rather than resolving conflicts on the shared branch, and three human-gated promotions (feature done → development; go to staging; go live → main). Deploy bindings (which branches auto-deploy to which environment) are a per-project parameter stated in the project's CLAUDE.md; this skill carries the durable method. Serving an in-flight worktree without merging it back is provided by the serve-worktree target in BeSpunky-scaffolded (Nx) projects.
